body:
Emily Long

                                                                        Freelance Writer
                                                            
            
            
        
            ExperienceEmily Long is a freelance writer based in Salt Lake City. After graduating from Duke University, she spent several years reporting on the federal workforce for Government Executive, a publication of Atlantic Media Company, in Washington, D.C. She has nearly a decade of experience as a freelancer covering tech (including issues related to security, privacy, and streaming) as well as personal finance and travel. In addition to Lifehacker, her work has been featured on Wirecutter, Tom’s Guide, and ZDNET. Emily has also worked as a travel guide around the U.S. and as a content editor. She has a masters in social work and is a licensed therapist in Utah.

        Netflix's July slate kicks off with the return of Millie Bobbie Brown as detective Enola Holmes—the third film in the series (aptly titled Enola Holmes 3) drops on July 1. The popular sports documentary series Quarterback (July 14) also returns for a third season, this time following Jayden Daniels, Baker Mayfield, Cam Ward, and Joe Flacco through the 2025 NFL season. New next month is Hot Ones: Extra Heat (July 13), which brings the popular YouTube series on location to sporting events and film premieres. Sean Evens will still be hosting and eating hot wings while interviewing guests. Meanwhile, Will Ferrell and Molly Shannon star in the new comedy series The Hawk (July 16), in which Ferrell plays a golf legend trying to win one final major. And Kevin Hart stars in 72 Hours (July 24), a Hangover-like film in which Hart's character joins a bachelor party after being added to the group chat by mistake. Finally, Netflix will be hosting the 2026 MLB Home Run Derby, which is set to stream live from Philadelphia's Citizens Bank Park on July 13 at 7 p.m. ET.

            Credit: RingConn
                            
    Table of Contents
    
        
        Tracking your health shouldn't mean strapping an intrusive, glowing smartwatch to your wrist 24/7. For a screen-free, low-profile alternative, the RingConn Gen 2 smart ring monitors your vitals right from your finger while dodging both premium hardware markups and the mandatory subscription fees that plague the wearable market. Right now, you can score this stealthy tracker for 30% off. Here is what the hardware brings to the table.
All of the info, none of the discomfortThe RingConn Gen 2 tracks the same data as a smartwatch—including activity like steps and calorie burn, pulse, heart rate variability, and blood oxygen levels. Designed for comfortable overnight wear and effortless 24/7 use, it is the first smart ring to offer sleep apnea monitoring while tracking your sleep quality, stress levels, and recovery. All of this data is compiled in the RingConn app, which delivers an overall Wellness Score and lets you export reports directly to your healthcare provider.If you have specific sleep issues you've dealt with in the past, or if you just want to do a better job tracking and incrementally improving both the duration and quality of your sleep, the small form factor of the RingConn Gen 2 and the granular data made available through the RingConn app offer a fantastic solution, giving you the necessary insights without obstructing your sleep with a bulky watch. No big battery drain for way less downtimeMost smart watches measure their battery life in hours. The Gen 2 can measure it in weeks, with a battery life of 10-12 days on a single charge for most sizes. Plus at just two grams, the Gen 2 is lighter than RingConn’s previous devices. All this makes it easier to use around the clock, and thanks to its titanium alloy construction, also surprisingly durable.
            
                What do you think so far?
            
        
No monthly subscription fees, just your dataAside from the discomfort and the near-constant charging, the worst part about most fitness trackers is seeing that monthly subscription fee hit your bank account. Nearly every health tracker requires an ongoing payment for full access to your device’s info and training insights, but Gen 2 users get access to all of their personal advanced health data with zero subscription required.If you want low-profile tracking without the distraction of an extra display, the RingConn Gen 2 is currently seeing a rare price drop. Amazon is slashing 30% off the MSRP during its Prime Day event running from June 23 through June 26. The wearable is also HSA/FSA eligible, allowing you to use tax-advantaged funds to secure the discount.

        Prime Day is June 23 to 26, and Lifehacker is sharing the best sales based on product reviews, comparisons, and price-tracking tools before it's over. Follow our live blog to stay up to date on the best sales we find.Browse our editors’ picks for a curated list of our favorite sales on laptops, fitness tech, appliances, and more.Subscribe to our shopping newsletter, Add to Cart, for the best sales sent to your inbox.New to Prime Day? We have a primer on everything you need to know. Sales are accurate at the time of publication, but prices and inventory are always subject to change.Now that the screenless Fitbit Air has made its debut, it's hardly surprising that older Fitbits are on sale. But I think the existence of the Air actually makes older Fitbits seem like an even better deal: They can do everything the Air can do, plus they have a screen to tell you the time and show you a few metrics during your workouts. You don't even have to pay extra for this functionality—for Prime Day, the Charge 6 and Inspire 3 are cheaper than the Air, and in fact, both are at their lowest prices ever. Here are the best Prime Day fitbit deals I'm seeing on day three of the sale.Fitbit Charge 6 Prime Day DealOriginal price: $159.95 | Current price: $75.99 (Save $84)The Charge 6 is the newest Fitbit (before the Air), and it is the one I'd recommend for most people. It's on sale for Prime for $75.99 in the red color, and $85.45 in black or off-white. It has its own GPS, so it can track runs even if you don't bring your phone. If you do bring your phone with you, though, you also have access to Google Pay, directions from Google Maps, and (with a subscription) YouTube Music. You can read my review of the Charge 6 here. Compared to fitness watches, it's not impressive, but for a minimalist tracker, it does a lot more than the Air. Fitbit Inspire 3 Prime Day DealOriginal price: $99.95 | Current price: $66.45 (Save $33)I used to think of the Inspire 3 as the Charge 6's less-capable sibling, but now I think of it as more of a Fitbit Air that also has a screen. It doesn't have GPS or payments, but it's a small wristband that can tell you the time and tell you your heart rate during workouts. The yellow and black versions are currently $66.45, and the lilac is just a few dollars more, at $69.95. Fitbit Versa 4 Prime Day DealOriginal price: $199.95 | Current price: $124.45 (Save $75)The Versa line is one of Fitbit's smartwatch devices, with a square screen that's a bit reminiscent of an Apple Watch. The Versa 4 is $124.45 in black, with other colors for a few extra dollars. The Versa 4 has its own GPS, can take calls and receive texts if your phone is in range, and has Google Wallet and Maps. 
            
                What do you think so far?
            
        
Fitbit Sense 2 Prime Day DealOriginal price: $249.95 | Current price: $197.90 (Save $52)The Sense 2 is technically not on a Prime Day deal; it's just on a long-running sale. The Sense 2 is also a Fitbit-styled smartwatch, similar to the Versa 4, but it adds ECG capabilities. That said, if you're looking for a square smartwatch that does more than a garden-variety Fitbit, perhaps you'd be better off with one of the Apple Watches that's on sale today—such as the SE 3 at $199. Looking for something else? Retailers like Walmart and Best Buy run Prime Day-style sales that are especially useful if you don’t have Amazon Prime.Walmart’s Prime Day competition sale runs from June 22-28 and includes deals up to 50% off. It’s an especially good option if you have Walmart+. Best Buy’s Prime Day competition sale runs from June 22-28, and has some of the best tech sales online. It’s an especially good option if you’re a My Best Buy “Plus” or “Total” member.Target’s Prime Day competition sale runs from June 23-26, with deals up to 50% off. Suunto Run Prime Day dealOriginal price: $199 | Current price: $148.99 (Save $50)The Suunto Run is a fantastic entry-level watch for runners and general fitness purposes. It also doesn't do heart rate tracking, but does include a compass, altimeter, and barometer. The battery lasts a year. It's more a watch than a smartwatch, but if that's what you're looking for, it's a good pick.Suunto 9 Peak Pro Prime Day dealOriginal price: $249 | Current price: $189.99 (Save $59)The Suunto 9 Peak Pro is similar to a MIP version of the Suunto Run and has a longer battery life—up to 21 days in smartwatch mode.

Sales are accurate at the time of publication, but prices and inventory are always subject to change. Despite all the great smart security cameras on the market right now, floodlight cameras are still the best option if you want a healthy dose of intruder deterrence and bright lighting alongside your video surveillance to eliminate dark hiding spots around your property. While they’re generally a bit pricier than regular security cams, the Blink Wired Floodlight Camera is currently 70% off, down to a record-low price of $29.99 (originally $99.99) for Prime Day.
This dual-LED wired model has 1080p video, color night vision, intelligent motion alerts, and a built-in siren. It’s compatible with third-party devices via IFTTT and Alexa voice controls, and is weather-resistant, though an IP rating isn’t stated. Along with a camera, the Blink Wired Floodlight features two LED floodlights with a combined brightness of 2,600 lumens (1,300 lumens each) with a 5,000K color temperature. This PCMag review, which gives it an "excellent" rating, notes that it's significantly brighter than the Blink Floodlight Mount, which doesn’t include a camera.Both the lights and camera connect to the included fixture with mounting arms that give users a solid range of tilt and swivel adjustability, and a 143-degree field of view to minimize blind spots and capture broad areas. Users can view up to five minutes of live video at a time and receive motion notifications for free. To unlock more features, a paid subscription is required, and a hub (sold separately) is required for local video storage.  
            
                What do you think so far?
            
        
Like other Blink devices, the camera can be controlled through the Blink mobile app, allowing you to livestream footage, take snapshots, and arm or disarm the system remotely. When armed, the camera automatically records video and sends alerts when motion is detected. At just $29.99, the Blink Wired Floodlight Camera is one of the cheapest ways to add bright floodlighting, video surveillance, and a built-in siren to your home's security setup.Looking for something else?             ExperiencePradershika Sharma is a tech deals writer for Lifehacker. She has a Master’s degree in English Literature, a B.Ed., and a TESOL certification. She has been writing professionally since 2018, creating product reviews, affiliate articles, and search ads for global clients while working with Rubix Agency and Cognizant.     Table of Contents
    
        
        Prime Day is June 23 to 26, and Lifehacker is sharing the best sales based on product reviews, comparisons, and price-tracking tools before it's over. Follow our live blog to stay up to date on the best sales we find.Browse our editors’ picks for a curated list of our favorite sales on laptops, fitness tech, appliances, and more.Subscribe to our shopping newsletter, Add to Cart, for the best sales sent to your inbox.New to Prime Day? We have a primer on everything you need to know. Sales are accurate at the time of publication, but prices and inventory are always subject to change. Right now, the Solix F3800 portable power station bundle is down to $1,999.98 from its usual $3,999 price. According to price trackers, that's the lowest price this package has reached so far. We're also tracking other notable discounts and buying advice across a wide range of categories for this year’s Prime Day sales event.
With a 3,840Wh battery and up to 6,000W of output, the F3800 is built for more than keeping phones and laptops charged during an outage. Most portable power stations are limited to standard 120V outlets, which covers TVs, computers, lights, and small appliances, but the F3800 also supports 240V output, allowing it to power larger appliances that many competing models simply can't handle. That includes electric dryers, ovens, certain air-conditioning systems and EVs, and an RV through its dedicated 30-amp outlet. Anker also designed the system with expansion in mind. You can add up to six expansion batteries, increasing total capacity to 26.88kWh.The included 400W solar panel also adds another layer of flexibility—if you're using the system for camping, RV travel, or extended outages, you can replenish some of that capacity without relying entirely on the electrical grid. The panel uses high-efficiency monocrystalline cells, offers adjustable stand angles to help capture more sunlight throughout the day, and carries an IP67 weather-resistance rating for outdoor use.
            
                What do you think so far?
            
        
The biggest drawback here is portability. Despite the wheels, the Solix F3800 weighs more than 130 pounds, so this isn't something you'll casually carry from place to place. It's better thought of as a movable backup-power system than a grab-and-go battery pack. It's also still a significant investment, even at half off.Looking for something else? Sales are accurate at the time of publication, but prices and inventory are always subject to change. Prime Day is here, and the deals and discounts are live, including this Blink bundle with a video doorbell, an Outdoor 4 camera, and a Sync Module Core for just $27.99. That's down from $139.98 and, according to price trackers, its lowest price ever. For apartments, townhomes, or smaller houses, it's a practical setup—the doorbell can cover your front entrance, while the included Outdoor 4 camera can monitor a second doorway, a driveway, a backyard gate, or any other area you want to keep an eye on. Amazon is also selling the Blink Video Doorbell with Sync Module Core separately for $23.99, down from $59.99, though spending an extra four dollars for an additional security camera feels like the more sensible option.

Also, while the system works well with Amazon Alexa, Echo Show displays, and Fire TV devices, support for Google Home and Apple HomeKit is absent. Sales are accurate at the time of publication, but prices and inventory are always subject to change. One of the easiest ways to improve your TV setup is to stop relying on the TV's built-in speakers, especially if you regularly struggle to hear dialogue. The Samsung HW-Q800H is designed with that in mind, and it's currently $727.99, down from its usual $1,097.99—the lowest price yet so far, according to price trackers. It's also one of the more notable home-theater deals we've spotted for Prime Day (we're tracking more deals and buying advice in our Prime Day 2026 coverage).
Its 5.1.2-channel setup consists of the main soundbar and a wireless subwoofer. More importantly, Samsung includes a dedicated center channel along with two separate dialogue enhancement modes, which makes conversations easier to hear during movies, sports broadcasts, and TV shows. Sales are accurate at the time of publication, but prices and inventory are always subject to change. Widely praised for its impressive battery life, the sleek Google Pixel 4 is one of the best mainstream fitness trackers and smart watches on the market, complete with built-in Gemini voice control. And for Prime Day, the Google Pixel Watch 4 (41mm) with an Iris Active Band and LTE is on sale for $345 (originally $449.99), bringing the price down by over $100. 
Complete with Google’s signature domed AMOLED display, the Pixel 4 has a sleeker look than many bulkier Android smartwatches and offers top-notch health and fitness tracking through Fitbit integration. Users can access sleep tracking, workouts, ECG, heart rate monitoring, and wellness insights. It also comes with useful safety add-ons like fall detection and emergency SOS features. One of the biggest upgrades over its predecessor is the display, which features slimmer bezels, a more usable screen area, and increased brightness of up to 3,000 nits, compared to 2,000 nits on the previous model.The main draw, however, is the improved battery life. According to this PCMag review, battery life lasts up to 56 hours in real-world testing with the always-on display enabled, outperforming similar flagship models from Apple and Samsung. The 41mm model now has a 325mAh battery (previously  306mAh) and recharges quickly, going from 5% remaining to full in just 43 minutes. Unlike most smartwatches, which use a flat magnetic puck, the Pixel 4 charges while resting on its side in the stand, allowing it to double as a bedside display. The watch's internal components and sensors have also been upgraded, including support for satellite communications through its new chipset. Dual-frequency GPS improves location tracking accuracy compared to the single-band signal on the Pixel Watch 3. To use the Pixel Watch 4, you’ll need a phone with Android 11 or newer, and some features on the phone, like camera controls and call screening, are exclusive to Pixel phones. That said, most of the tracking capabilities are compatible with any Android phone.

If you’re an Android user who wants easy access to Gemini alongside a comprehensive range of lifestyle and fitness features, the stylish Google Pixel Watch 4, with a curved display and impressive battery life, is one of the top contenders if you’re in the market for a premium Android smartwatch, especially at over $100 off.Looking for something else? The brand is offering deals on several of its bestsellers, which allow you to remain aware of your surroundings while listening to music or podcasts or taking calls. The current discounts knock up to 40% off several models, including some newly released.
The new OpenDots ONE are $60 offThe OpenDots ONE are 30% off, dropping down to $139.95 (originally $199.95). One of Shokz’s newest designs, the OpenDots ONE are clip-on earbuds that sit outside the ear canal, offering a lightweight alternative to traditional earbuds while maintaining situational awareness (they're a good alternative to the Bose Ultra open earbuds). The OpenRun Pro 2 have also been discounted $60 For runners, the OpenRun Pro 2 are down to $119.95 (originally $179.95). This beloved bone-conduction model combines Shokz’s signature open-ear design with a bassy sound and long battery life (up to 12 hours), making it one of the brand’s most popular choices for outdoor workouts. The regular OpenRun headphones are also discounted, dropping down to $89.95 (originally $129.95). body:
Becca Lewis
                            
        
            Becca Lewis
                
                                                                        Editorial Author
                                                            
            
            
        
            ExperienceBecca Lewis is home maintenance writer who aims to foster confidence in and inspire DIY enthusiasts at all skill levels. Becca attended Southern Connecticut State University, where she studied technical theater and design. She has a background in carpentry and fabrication and has worked in specialty shops, using a wide variety of tools and materials, for more than 20 years. Becca has also written for Angi, where she specializes in creating guides for DIY, home maintenance, and renovations, and she indulges her love of sci-fi and fantasy by writing about entertainment and science for Giant Freakin Robot. She serves on the board of the Good Junk Sanctuary in Portland, Oregon, focusing on community engagement in the DIY and reuse space.

        Prime Day is June 23 to 26, and Lifehacker is sharing the best sales based on product reviews, comparisons, and price-tracking tools before it's over. Follow our live blog to stay up to date on the best sales we find.Browse our editors’ picks for a curated list of our favorite sales on laptops, fitness tech, appliances, and more.Subscribe to our shopping newsletter, Add to Cart, for the best sales sent to your inbox.New to Prime Day? We have a primer on everything you need to know. Sales are accurate at the time of publication, but prices and inventory are always subject to change.If you have DIY projects to tackle this summer, having quality tools on hand is essential. DeWalt tools are known and trusted by pros for their reliability and durability, and I have quite a few of them in my professional set at work. But if you’re filling out your own tool kit, you have no doubt noticed that tools can also be quite expensive. Luckily, Amazon Prime Day includes some great discounts on DeWalt tools and accessories—you can save as much as 50%. Here are some of my top picks to consider.The best Prime Day deals on DeWalt Cordless ToolsIf you want to do some DIY woodworking or undertake a basic project like hanging shelves, having a few simple cordless tools will make the job quicker to complete and the results look more professional. Choosing a brand like DeWalt, which has over 300 cordless tools that work with their 20-volt batteries, means that you can build out your tool kit with batteries that will grow alongside your skills and abilities, and they will last for up to a decade before needing to be replaced. This DeWalt 20-volt drill is on sale for $129, 46% off its regular price. A drill is the cornerstone of any DIY cordless tool set, and can be used for drilling holes and driving fasteners as well as with bits for brushes and other finishing attachments. This is a tool-only deal, so you’ll need a DeWalt 20-volt battery and charger to use it.This DeWalt 20-volt, 2-gallon wet-dry cordless shop vac is on sale for $119, 32% off its usual price. It comes with a hose and filter. Although this shop vac doesn’t come with a battery, you can use it with a cord, so you don’t necessarily need a battery for it to work. A wet-dry shop vac is useful for many plumbing projects as well as to clean up around your home workshop.This DeWalt 20-volt drill and circular saw combo kit is on sale for $199, 33% off its typical price. This set comes with a drill, a circular saw, two 2-amp-hour batteries, a charger, and a tool bag. This is a good set for budding DIYers because it includes batteries, although if you plan on using the saw for more than a few cuts at a time, you will likely want to invest in a higher-capacity battery.The best Prime Day deals on DeWalt batteriesBatteries will make or break your cordless tool set, and having enough of them on hand so you never have to wait for one to charge will make your DIY projects go much more smoothly. 
            
                What do you think so far?
            
        
A set of 4 DeWalt 20-volt MAX batteries is on sale for $129, 28% off its regular price. It includes two 2-amp-hour batteries and two 4-amp-hour batteries. This is a good expansion pack for an existing DeWalat set, but it doesn’t come with a charger, so you’ll need to buy one if you don’t already have a DeWalt 20-volt charger.This set of 4 DeWalt 20-volt MAX XR batteries is on sale for $329, 30% off its usual price. It comes with two 3.5-amp-hour batteries and two 1.7-amp-hour batteries. These are compact, light, and efficient batteries. The advantage of this battery type is that it lasts longer on a charge and has higher output burst than a regular 20-volt battery. Because they're light, they’re more comfortable to use for long periods of time, and you can keep your tools going for longer on a single charge. (These are good batteries for upgrading your existing DeWalt 20-volt tool set, but there's no charger included.)The best Prime Day deals on DeWalt hand tools and bitsHaving the right size socket or bit when you need it makes the difference between an enjoyable DIY experience and an exercise in frustration. Here are some deals on DeWalt hand tools and accessories.This DeWalt 192-piece mechanic’s tool set is on sale for $156.74, 46% off its typical price. It comes with ½-inch, ¼-inc, and ⅜-inch ratchet handles, sockets in a range of SAE and metric sizes, a set of ratchet extensions, various adapters, a set of alan keys, and a tool case. This is an especially good starter kit if you’re interested in vehicle maintenance or home appliance maintenance.The 100-piece DeWalt impact driver bit set is on sale for $32.98, 50% off its regular price. This set includes bits for drilling holes and driving fasteners, plus a magnetic screw-holder extension. This is a good set for most DIY tasks that you would use a drill or impact driver for. (The bits can be used with either a drill or an impact driver because of the way the shaft is designed.)Looking for something else? title: Apple Just Raised Prices on These Devices by Hundreds of Dollars, but Many Are Still Discounted on Prime Day
body:
Jake Peterson
                            
        
            Jake Peterson
                
                                            Senior Technology Editor
                                    
            
            
        
            ExperienceJake Peterson is Lifehacker’s Tech Editor, and has been covering tech news and how-tos for nearly a decade. His team covers all things technology, including AI, smartphones, computers, game consoles, and subscriptions.
        
        
            Areas of Expertise
        
        Read Full Bio
    

    
                                        
        
        
        June 25, 2026
                
        Add as a preferred source on Google

    
    
        Add as a preferred source on Google

    We may earn a commission from links on this page.
            Deal pricing and availability subject to change after time of publication.
    
            Credit: Amazon/Lifehacker
                            
    Table of Contents
    
        
        Apple products are expensive, and they always have been. That's no secret. But some of them just got a lot more expensive: On Thursday morning, Apple announced a series of price hikes on key products in its lineup, affecting Macs and MacBooks, iPads, Apple TVs, HomePods, and Vision Pro. If you're in the market for a new Apple laptop or tablet, that market just got a good deal pricier. Which Apple products are now more expensive?Visit Apple's official website, or the Apple Store in person, and it won't look like anything's different. But if you compare today's prices on the following items to yesterday's, you'll notice some seismic changes:MacBook Neo: $699 (previously $599)MacBook Air (13-inch): $1,299 (previously $1,099)MacBook Air (15-inch): $1,499 (previously $1,299)MacBook Pro (M5): $1,999 (previously $1,699)MacBook Pro (M5 Pro): $2,499 (previously $2,199)MacBook Pro (M5 Max): $4,099 (previously $3,599)iMac: $1,499 (previously $1,299)Mac Studio (M4 Max): $2,499 (previously $1,999)Mac Studio (M3 Ultra): $5,299 (previously $3,999)iPad (A16): $449 (previously $349)iPad Air (11-inch): $749 (previously $599)iPad Air (13-inch): $949 (previously $749)iPad Pro (11-inch): $1,199 (previously $999)iPad Pro (13-inch): $1,499 (previously $1,299)iPad mini: $599 (previously $499)Apple TV 4K: $199 (previously $129)HomePod: $349 (previously $299)HomePod mini: $129 (previously $99)Vision Pro: $3,699 (previously $3,499)These are not small increases. Macs cost anywhere from $200 to $500 more on Thursday than they did on Wednesday; while iPads are $100 to $200 more. Perhaps most strikingly, the Apple TV is $70 more, which represents a 54% increase. While Apple's streaming device is among the best (and certainly the most private) on the market, it is now one of, if not the most expensive option. I'm not sure how many people will choose it at $199 when plenty of alternatives exist for a fraction of the cost. The increases themselves are not a surprise, however. Outgoing Apple CEO Tim Cook announced last week that the company was being forced to raise prices, as the global memory crisis is putting a strain on the cost of components and products everywhere. Still, I'm don't think anyone predicted how sharply Apple would boost prices. It's a sign of the scope of the crisis, and a look at what Apple thinks it needs to do to keep growing its profits. (The company is now worth $4.3 trillion.)You can still get Apple devices on sale from Amazon during Prime DayWhile this news is rough for anyone looking for a new Apple device (particularly one of the devices listed above), there is a silver lining: This week is Amazon Prime Day, and as luck would have it, a number of the devices affected by the price increases are actually on sale. 
            
                What do you think so far?
            
        
Not every item affected by the increases can be found at a discount—some are just available at the previous MSRP, but all of the following items are much cheaper on Amazon than at Apple right now:MacBook Neo: $589.99 (compared to $699 direct from Apple) MacBook Air (13-inch): $949 (compared to $1,299) MacBook Air (15-inch): $1,149.99 (compared to $1,499)MacBook Pro (M5): $1,549 (compared to $1,999)MacBook Pro (M5 Pro): $2,034 (compared to $2,499) MacBook Pro (M5 Max): $3,299.99 (compared to $4,099)iMac: $1,149 (compared to $1,499)iPad (A16): $299 (compared to $449)iPad Air (11-inch): $519 (compared to $749)Apple TV 4K (64GB renewed): $154 (compared to $199)HomePod: $299 (previously $349)Now that Apple has officially changed the prices of these items, their price tags on Amazon are likely to increase soon as well. As such, if you're interested in any of them, I'd encourage you to buy sooner rather than later. As today's hikes proved, there's no telling when they might cost even more.
